Some control variables, notably homeownership and marriage/cohabitation, lose significance with weighting, likely because demographic reweighting adjusts the sample composition. These are controls rather than focal predictors, so this does not affect the substantive conclusions.

The network consequence models (Tables S25-S26) are more sensitive to weighting, as expected given limited within-person variation with only three waves. In the fixed effects models (Table S25), the within-Bay association with local non-kin gains and the out-of-Bay association with distant non-kin gains both lose significance, though the out-of-Bay association with distant kin remains significant. The first-difference models (Table S26) are more stable: the finding that out-of-Bay movers show declining distant ties one wave later holds with weighting (distant non-kin: -0.676**, distant kin: -0.555*). Overall, the pattern of network adjustment after moving is consistent across specifications, while the point estimates of immediate network change are less precisely estimated with weights.

```{r wtd-setup}
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(plm)

data_path <- here("data", "out", "R_DF.rds")
if (!file.exists(data_path)) data_path <- here("data", "R_DF.rds")
wtd_data_available <- file.exists(data_path)
if (wtd_data_available) {
  df_wtd <- readRDS(data_path)
  # Drop observations with missing weights
  df_wtd <- df_wtd %>% filter(!is.na(wt_dem_95_inf) & is.finite(wt_dem_95_inf))
}

# Shared variable labels
cs_labels_w <- c(
  "(Intercept)"        = "Constant",
  "Kin_within_60_N"    = "Kin within 60 min",
  "Nonkin_within_60_N" = "Non-kin within 60 min",
  "Kin_in_HH_N"        = "Kin in Household",
  "Nonkin_in_HH_N"     = "Non-kin in Household",
  "Kin_GT_Hour_N"      = "Kin >60 min away",
  "Nonkin_GT_Hour_N"   = "Non-kin >60 min away",
  "Owns_home"          = "Home Ownership",
  "Full_Time"          = "Full-Time Employment",
  "BA_or_greater"      = "BA or Greater",
  "Marr_Cohab"         = "Married/Cohabiting",
  "LE_NewJob"          = "New Job",
  "LE_NewSchool"       = "New School",
  "LEX_Birth"          = "Birth of Child",
  "LE_FinanProb"       = "Financial Problems",
  "black_race"         = "Black",
  "asian_race"         = "Asian",
  "other_race"         = "Other Race",
  "latino_eth"         = "Latino/a",
  "female"             = "Female",
  "age"                = "Age"
)

# Shared formatting function for flextable styling
style_table <- function(ft, section_rows, n_row) {
  ft <- theme_booktabs(ft)
  ft <- bold(ft, i = section_rows, j = 1, part = "body")
  if (length(n_row) > 0) {
    ft <- hline(ft, i = n_row - 1, part = "body", border = fp_border(width = 0.5))
  }
  ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 9, part = "all")
  ft <- font(ft, fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all")
  ft <- align(ft, j = 1, align = "left", part = "body")
  ft <- align(ft, j = -1, align = "center", part = "body")
  ft <- align(ft, align = "center", part = "header")
  ft <- bold(ft, part = "header")
  ft
}

style_footer <- function(ft, note) {
  ft <- add_footer_lines(ft, values = note)
  ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 9, part = "footer")
  ft <- font(ft, fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "footer")
  ft <- italic(ft, part = "footer")
  ft
}
```

```{r tbl-s23-plan-weighted, eval=wtd_data_available}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table S23: Weighted Table 1 — Plan to Move (LPM)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
predictors_w <- c(
  "Kin_within_60_N", "Nonkin_within_60_N",
  "Kin_in_HH_N", "Nonkin_in_HH_N",
  "Kin_GT_Hour_N", "Nonkin_GT_Hour_N",
  "Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab",
  "LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb",
  "black_race", "asian_race", "other_race", "latino_eth", "female", "age"
)

formula_w <- paste("Plan_to_Move ~", paste(predictors_w, collapse = " + "), "+ factor(WAVE)")
all_w <- df_wtd[complete.cases(df_wtd[, c("Plan_to_Move", predictors_w, "prim_key", "agegroup", "WAVE")]), ]

run_lpm_w <- function(data) {
  fit <- lm(as.formula(formula_w), data = data, weights = wt_dem_95_inf)
  cl_se <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit, cluster = data$prim_key)))
  coefs <- coef(fit)
  t_stats <- coefs / cl_se
  p_vals <- 2 * pt(abs(t_stats), df = fit$df.residual, lower.tail = FALSE)
  stars <- ifelse(p_vals < 0.01, "***",
           ifelse(p_vals < 0.05, "**",
           ifelse(p_vals < 0.10, "*", "")))
  list(coefs = coefs, se = cl_se, stars = stars,
       n = nobs(fit), n_clusters = n_distinct(data$prim_key),
       r2 = summary(fit)$r.squared)
}

mw_all   <- run_lpm_w(all_w)
mw_young <- run_lpm_w(all_w %>% filter(agegroup == 0))
mw_old   <- run_lpm_w(all_w %>% filter(agegroup == 1))

fmt_w <- function(m, var) {
  if (var %in% names(m$coefs)) {
    sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", m$coefs[var], m$stars[var], m$se[var])
  } else { "" }
}

network_w <- c("Kin_within_60_N", "Nonkin_within_60_N", "Kin_in_HH_N",
               "Nonkin_in_HH_N", "Kin_GT_Hour_N", "Nonkin_GT_Hour_N")
ses_w <- c("Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab")
events_w <- c("LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb")
demo_w <- c("black_race", "asian_race", "other_race", "latino_eth", "female", "age")
ordered_w <- c(network_w, ses_w, events_w, demo_w)

sections_w <- list(
  list(before = network_w[1], label = "Network Variables"),
  list(before = ses_w[1],     label = "Socioeconomic Status"),
  list(before = events_w[1],  label = "Life Events"),
  list(before = demo_w[1],    label = "Demographics")
)

rows_w <- list()
for (var in ordered_w) {
  for (s in sections_w) {
    if (var == s$before) {
      rows_w[[length(rows_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Variable = s$label, All = "", Young = "", Old = "",
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  label <- ifelse(var %in% names(cs_labels_w), cs_labels_w[var], var)
  label <- paste0("  ", label)
  rows_w[[length(rows_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    All   = fmt_w(mw_all, var),
    Young = fmt_w(mw_young, var),
    Old   = fmt_w(mw_old, var),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
}
rows_w[[length(rows_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (person-waves)",
  All = as.character(mw_all$n), Young = as.character(mw_young$n),
  Old = as.character(mw_old$n), stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
rows_w[[length(rows_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (individuals)",
  All = as.character(mw_all$n_clusters), Young = as.character(mw_young$n_clusters),
  Old = as.character(mw_old$n_clusters), stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
rows_w[[length(rows_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "R-squared",
  All = sprintf("%.3f", mw_all$r2), Young = sprintf("%.3f", mw_young$r2),
  Old = sprintf("%.3f", mw_old$r2), stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

result_w1 <- do.call(rbind, rows_w)
sec_w1 <- which(result_w1$Variable %in% c("Network Variables", "Socioeconomic Status",
                                            "Life Events", "Demographics"))
n_w1 <- which(result_w1$Variable == "N (person-waves)")

ft_w1 <- flextable(result_w1)
ft_w1 <- set_header_labels(ft_w1, Variable = "", All = "(1) Whole Sample",
                            Young = "(2) Young Adults", Old = "(3) Older Adults")
ft_w1 <- style_table(ft_w1, sec_w1, n_w1)
ft_w1 <- set_caption(ft_w1, caption = "Table S23. Weighted Predictors of Planning to Move (cf. Table 1)")
ft_w1 <- style_footer(ft_w1, paste(
  "Weighted linear probability model with wave fixed effects.",
  "Demographic survey weights (wt_dem_95_inf) applied.",
  "Cluster-robust standard errors (by individual) in parentheses.",
  "This table replicates Table 1 with survey weights to assess robustness.",
  "* p < 0.10, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01."
))
ft_w1 <- autofit(ft_w1)
ft_w1
```

```{r tbl-s24-mobility-weighted, eval=wtd_data_available}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table S24: Weighted Table 2 — Mobility outcomes, young adults (LPM)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
young_w <- df_wtd %>% filter(agegroup == 0)
base_preds_w <- predictors_w

# Model 1: Any Move
any_fw <- paste("Moved_w23 ~", paste(base_preds_w, collapse = " + "), "+ factor(WAVE)")
any_dw <- young_w[complete.cases(young_w[, c("Moved_w23", base_preds_w, "prim_key", "WAVE")]), ]
fit_any_w <- lm(as.formula(any_fw), data = any_dw, weights = wt_dem_95_inf)
se_any_w <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit_any_w, cluster = any_dw$prim_key)))

# Model 2: Out-of-Bay
out_fw <- paste("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23 ~", paste(base_preds_w, collapse = " + "), "+ factor(WAVE)")
out_dw <- young_w[complete.cases(young_w[, c("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23", base_preds_w, "prim_key", "WAVE")]), ]
fit_out_w <- lm(as.formula(out_fw), data = out_dw, weights = wt_dem_95_inf)
se_out_w <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit_out_w, cluster = out_dw$prim_key)))

# Model 3: Within-Bay (exclude out-of-Bay movers)
young_in_w <- young_w %>%
  filter(Moved_out_of_Bay_w23 != 1 | is.na(Moved_out_of_Bay_w23))
in_fw <- paste("Moved_in_Bay_w23 ~", paste(base_preds_w, collapse = " + "), "+ factor(WAVE)")
in_dw <- young_in_w[complete.cases(young_in_w[, c("Moved_in_Bay_w23", base_preds_w, "prim_key", "WAVE")]), ]
fit_in_w <- lm(as.formula(in_fw), data = in_dw, weights = wt_dem_95_inf)
se_in_w <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit_in_w, cluster = in_dw$prim_key)))

fmt_cell_w <- function(fit, se, var) {
  if (!(var %in% names(coef(fit)))) return("")
  b <- coef(fit)[var]; s <- se[var]
  p <- 2 * pt(abs(b / s), df = fit$df.residual, lower.tail = FALSE)
  star <- ifelse(p < 0.01, "***", ifelse(p < 0.05, "**", ifelse(p < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", b, star, s)
}

rows_w2 <- list()
for (var in ordered_w) {
  for (s in sections_w) {
    if (var == s$before) {
      rows_w2[[length(rows_w2) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Variable = s$label, Any = "", Out = "", In = "",
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  label <- ifelse(var %in% names(cs_labels_w), cs_labels_w[var], var)
  label <- paste0("  ", label)
  rows_w2[[length(rows_w2) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    Any = fmt_cell_w(fit_any_w, se_any_w, var),
    Out = fmt_cell_w(fit_out_w, se_out_w, var),
    In  = fmt_cell_w(fit_in_w, se_in_w, var),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
}
rows_w2[[length(rows_w2) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (person-waves)",
  Any = as.character(nobs(fit_any_w)),
  Out = as.character(nobs(fit_out_w)),
  In  = as.character(nobs(fit_in_w)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
rows_w2[[length(rows_w2) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (individuals)",
  Any = as.character(n_distinct(any_dw$prim_key)),
  Out = as.character(n_distinct(out_dw$prim_key)),
  In  = as.character(n_distinct(in_dw$prim_key)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
rows_w2[[length(rows_w2) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "R-squared",
  Any = sprintf("%.3f", summary(fit_any_w)$r.squared),
  Out = sprintf("%.3f", summary(fit_out_w)$r.squared),
  In  = sprintf("%.3f", summary(fit_in_w)$r.squared),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

result_w2 <- do.call(rbind, rows_w2)
sec_w2 <- which(result_w2$Variable %in% c("Network Variables", "Socioeconomic Status",
                                            "Life Events", "Demographics"))
n_w2 <- which(result_w2$Variable == "N (person-waves)")

ft_w2 <- flextable(result_w2)
ft_w2 <- set_header_labels(ft_w2, Variable = "",
  Any = "(1) Any Move", Out = "(2) Out of Bay", In = "(3) Within Bay")
ft_w2 <- style_table(ft_w2, sec_w2, n_w2)
ft_w2 <- set_caption(ft_w2, caption = "Table S24. Weighted Predictors of Residential Mobility, Young Adults (cf. Table 2)")
ft_w2 <- style_footer(ft_w2, paste(
  "Weighted linear probability model with wave fixed effects, young adults (ages 21-30) only.",
  "Demographic survey weights (wt_dem_95_inf) applied.",
  "Cluster-robust standard errors (by individual) in parentheses.",
  "Model (1): any move vs. no move. Model (2): out-of-Bay vs. all who remained.",
  "Model (3): within-Bay movers vs. stayers (out-of-Bay movers excluded).",
  "This table replicates Table 2 with survey weights to assess robustness.",
  "* p < 0.10, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01."
))
ft_w2 <- autofit(ft_w2)
ft_w2
```

```{r tbl-s25-fe-weighted, eval=wtd_data_available}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table S25: Weighted Table 3 — Network consequences of moving (FE)
# plm does not support weights directly, so we use lm with individual dummies
# and weights, which is algebraically equivalent to weighted within-estimator
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
young_fe_w <- df_wtd %>% filter(agegroup == 0)

net_outcomes_w <- c("Nonkin_within_60_N", "Kin_within_60_N",
                    "Nonkin_GT_Hour_N", "Kin_GT_Hour_N")
net_col_labels_w <- c("Local Non-Kin", "Local Kin", "Distant Non-Kin", "Distant Kin")

all_predictors_w <- c("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23", "Moved_in_Bay_w23",
                       "Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab",
                       "LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb",
                       "Plan_to_Move")

pred_labels_w <- c(
  "Moved_out_of_Bay_w23" = "Moved Out of Bay",
  "Moved_in_Bay_w23"     = "Moved Within Bay",
  "Owns_home"            = "Home Ownership",
  "Full_Time"            = "Full-Time Employment",
  "BA_or_greater"        = "BA or Greater",
  "Marr_Cohab"           = "Married/Cohabiting",
  "LE_NewJob"            = "New Job",
  "LE_NewSchool"         = "New School",
  "LEX_Birth"            = "Birth of Child",
  "LE_FinanProb"         = "Financial Problems",
  "Plan_to_Move"         = "Plan to Move"
)

run_full_fe_w <- function(outcome, predictors, data) {
  all_vars <- c(outcome, predictors, "prim_key", "WAVE", "wt_dem_95_inf_w3")
  d <- data[complete.cases(data[, all_vars]), ]
  # Weighted FE via lm with factor(prim_key)
  f <- as.formula(paste(outcome, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "),
                        "+ factor(prim_key)"))
  fit <- tryCatch(lm(f, data = d, weights = wt_dem_95_inf_w3),
                  error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  rob_se <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit, cluster = d$prim_key)))
  coefs <- coef(fit)
  # Keep only predictor coefficients (not individual dummies)
  keep <- intersect(predictors, names(coefs))
  coefs <- coefs[keep]
  rob_se <- rob_se[keep]
  p_vals <- 2 * pt(abs(coefs / rob_se), df = fit$df.residual, lower.tail = FALSE)
  stars <- ifelse(p_vals < 0.01, "***", ifelse(p_vals < 0.05, "**", ifelse(p_vals < 0.10, "*", "")))
  list(coefs = coefs, se = rob_se, stars = stars,
       n = nrow(d), n_ind = length(unique(d$prim_key)))
}

fmt_full_w <- function(model, var) {
  if (is.null(model) || !(var %in% names(model$coefs))) return("")
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", model$coefs[var], model$stars[var], model$se[var])
}

models_w <- lapply(net_outcomes_w, function(dv) run_full_fe_w(dv, all_predictors_w, young_fe_w))

move_vars_w <- c("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23", "Moved_in_Bay_w23")
control_vars_w <- c("Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab",
                     "LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb", "Plan_to_Move")
sections_fe_w <- list(
  list(before = move_vars_w[1], label = "Move Indicators"),
  list(before = control_vars_w[1], label = "Controls")
)
ordered_preds_w <- c(move_vars_w, control_vars_w)

rows_fe_w <- list()
for (var in ordered_preds_w) {
  for (s in sections_fe_w) {
    if (var == s$before) {
      rows_fe_w[[length(rows_fe_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Variable = s$label, C1 = "", C2 = "", C3 = "", C4 = "",
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  label <- paste0("  ", pred_labels_w[var])
  rows_fe_w[[length(rows_fe_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    C1 = fmt_full_w(models_w[[1]], var), C2 = fmt_full_w(models_w[[2]], var),
    C3 = fmt_full_w(models_w[[3]], var), C4 = fmt_full_w(models_w[[4]], var),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
}
rows_fe_w[[length(rows_fe_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (person-waves)",
  C1 = as.character(models_w[[1]]$n), C2 = as.character(models_w[[2]]$n),
  C3 = as.character(models_w[[3]]$n), C4 = as.character(models_w[[4]]$n),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
rows_fe_w[[length(rows_fe_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N (individuals)",
  C1 = as.character(models_w[[1]]$n_ind), C2 = as.character(models_w[[2]]$n_ind),
  C3 = as.character(models_w[[3]]$n_ind), C4 = as.character(models_w[[4]]$n_ind),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

result_fe_w <- do.call(rbind, rows_fe_w)
sec_fe_w <- which(result_fe_w$Variable %in% c("Move Indicators", "Controls"))
n_fe_w <- which(result_fe_w$Variable == "N (person-waves)")

ft_fe_w <- flextable(result_fe_w)
ft_fe_w <- set_header_labels(ft_fe_w, Variable = "",
  C1 = "(1)", C2 = "(2)", C3 = "(3)", C4 = "(4)")
ft_fe_w <- add_header_row(ft_fe_w,
  values = c("", "Local", "Distant"),
  colwidths = c(1, 2, 2), top = TRUE)
ft_fe_w <- add_header_row(ft_fe_w,
  values = c("", net_col_labels_w),
  colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1), top = FALSE)
ft_fe_w <- style_table(ft_fe_w, sec_fe_w, n_fe_w)
ft_fe_w <- set_caption(ft_fe_w, caption = "Table S25. Weighted Network Changes Associated with Moving (Fixed Effects, Young Adults; cf. Table 3)")
ft_fe_w <- style_footer(ft_fe_w, paste(
  "Weighted individual fixed effects models. Young adults only.",
  "Demographic survey weights (wt_dem_95_inf_w3, person-level) applied.",
  "Each column is a separate model with the network variable as the DV.",
  "Cluster-robust SEs in parentheses.",
  "This table replicates Table 3 with survey weights to assess robustness.",
  "* p < 0.10, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01."
))
ft_fe_w <- autofit(ft_fe_w)
ft_fe_w
```

```{r tbl-s26-fd-weighted, eval=wtd_data_available}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table S26: Weighted Table 4 — First-difference network changes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
w2_w <- df_wtd %>% filter(agegroup == 0, WAVE == 2)
w3_w <- df_wtd %>% filter(agegroup == 0, WAVE == 3)

fd_w <- inner_join(
  w2_w %>% select(prim_key, Moved_out_of_Bay_w23, Moved_in_Bay_w23,
                   Nonkin_within_60_N, Nonkin_GT_Hour_N,
                   Kin_within_60_N, Kin_GT_Hour_N,
                   Owns_home, Full_Time, BA_or_greater, Marr_Cohab,
                   LE_NewJob, LE_NewSchool, LEX_Birth, LE_FinanProb,
                   Plan_to_Move, wt_dem_95_inf_w3),
  w3_w %>% select(prim_key,
                   Nonkin_within_60_N, Nonkin_GT_Hour_N,
                   Kin_within_60_N, Kin_GT_Hour_N),
  by = "prim_key", suffix = c("_w2", "_w3")
) %>%
  mutate(
    d_nonkin_local = Nonkin_within_60_N_w3 - Nonkin_within_60_N_w2,
    d_kin_local    = Kin_within_60_N_w3 - Kin_within_60_N_w2,
    d_nonkin_dist  = Nonkin_GT_Hour_N_w3 - Nonkin_GT_Hour_N_w2,
    d_kin_dist     = Kin_GT_Hour_N_w3 - Kin_GT_Hour_N_w2
  )

fd_dvs_w <- c("d_nonkin_local", "d_kin_local", "d_nonkin_dist", "d_kin_dist")
fd_col_labels_w <- c("Local Non-Kin", "Local Kin", "Distant Non-Kin", "Distant Kin")

fd_predictors_w <- c("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23", "Moved_in_Bay_w23",
                      "Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab",
                      "LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb",
                      "Plan_to_Move")

run_full_fd_w <- function(dv, predictors, data) {
  f <- as.formula(paste(dv, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
  d <- data[complete.cases(data[, c(dv, predictors, "prim_key", "wt_dem_95_inf_w3")]), ]
  d <- d[is.finite(d$wt_dem_95_inf_w3), ]
  fit <- lm(f, data = d, weights = wt_dem_95_inf_w3)
  rob_se <- sqrt(diag(vcovCL(fit, cluster = d$prim_key)))
  coefs <- coef(fit)
  coefs <- coefs[names(coefs) != "(Intercept)"]
  rob_se <- rob_se[names(rob_se) != "(Intercept)"]
  p_vals <- 2 * pt(abs(coefs / rob_se), df = fit$df.residual, lower.tail = FALSE)
  stars <- ifelse(p_vals < 0.01, "***", ifelse(p_vals < 0.05, "**", ifelse(p_vals < 0.10, "*", "")))
  list(coefs = coefs, se = rob_se, stars = stars, n = nobs(fit))
}

fmt_fd_w <- function(model, var) {
  if (is.null(model) || !(var %in% names(model$coefs))) return("")
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", model$coefs[var], model$stars[var], model$se[var])
}

fd_models_w <- lapply(fd_dvs_w, function(dv) run_full_fd_w(dv, fd_predictors_w, fd_w))

move_vars_fd_w <- c("Moved_out_of_Bay_w23", "Moved_in_Bay_w23")
control_vars_fd_w <- c("Owns_home", "Full_Time", "BA_or_greater", "Marr_Cohab",
                        "LE_NewJob", "LE_NewSchool", "LEX_Birth", "LE_FinanProb", "Plan_to_Move")
sections_fd_w <- list(
  list(before = move_vars_fd_w[1], label = "Move Indicators (wave 2)"),
  list(before = control_vars_fd_w[1], label = "Controls (wave 2)")
)
ordered_fd_w <- c(move_vars_fd_w, control_vars_fd_w)

rows_fd_w <- list()
for (var in ordered_fd_w) {
  for (s in sections_fd_w) {
    if (var == s$before) {
      rows_fd_w[[length(rows_fd_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Variable = s$label, C1 = "", C2 = "", C3 = "", C4 = "",
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
  label <- paste0("  ", pred_labels_w[var])
  rows_fd_w[[length(rows_fd_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = label,
    C1 = fmt_fd_w(fd_models_w[[1]], var), C2 = fmt_fd_w(fd_models_w[[2]], var),
    C3 = fmt_fd_w(fd_models_w[[3]], var), C4 = fmt_fd_w(fd_models_w[[4]], var),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
}
rows_fd_w[[length(rows_fd_w) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = "N",
  C1 = as.character(fd_models_w[[1]]$n), C2 = as.character(fd_models_w[[2]]$n),
  C3 = as.character(fd_models_w[[3]]$n), C4 = as.character(fd_models_w[[4]]$n),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

result_fd_w <- do.call(rbind, rows_fd_w)
sec_fd_w <- which(result_fd_w$Variable %in% c("Move Indicators (wave 2)", "Controls (wave 2)"))
n_fd_w <- which(result_fd_w$Variable == "N")

ft_fd_w <- flextable(result_fd_w)
ft_fd_w <- set_header_labels(ft_fd_w, Variable = "",
  C1 = "(1)", C2 = "(2)", C3 = "(3)", C4 = "(4)")
ft_fd_w <- add_header_row(ft_fd_w,
  values = c("", "Local", "Distant"),
  colwidths = c(1, 2, 2), top = TRUE)
ft_fd_w <- add_header_row(ft_fd_w,
  values = c("", fd_col_labels_w),
  colwidths = c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1), top = FALSE)
ft_fd_w <- style_table(ft_fd_w, sec_fd_w, n_fd_w)
ft_fd_w <- set_caption(ft_fd_w, caption = "Table S26. Weighted Longer-Term Network Changes (First-Difference, Young Adults; cf. Table 4)")
ft_fd_w <- style_footer(ft_fd_w, paste(
  "Weighted first-difference models. DV = change in network ties from wave 2 to wave 3.",
  "IVs = moved between wave 1 and wave 2 + wave-2 controls.",
  "Demographic survey weights (wt_dem_95_inf_w3, person-level) applied.",
  "Young adults only. Cluster-robust SEs (by individual) in parentheses.",
  "This table replicates Table 4 with survey weights to assess robustness.",
  "* p < 0.10, ** p < 0.05, *** p < 0.01."
))
ft_fd_w <- autofit(ft_fd_w)
ft_fd_w
```

## Appendix F: Reference

### F.1 Respondent-Level Descriptive Table

Table S27 presents the same descriptive comparison as Table 1, but at the respondent level (one row per person). Characteristics are measured at wave 1 (baseline) and move status is classified by whether the respondent ever moved out of the Bay Area, ever moved locally, or never moved during the study.

```{r tbl-s27-respondent-desc, eval=TRUE}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table S27: Respondent-level baseline characteristics by subsequent move status
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
data_path <- here("data", "out", "R_DF.rds")
if (!file.exists(data_path)) data_path <- here("data", "R_DF.rds")

if (file.exists(data_path)) {
  df_s27 <- readRDS(data_path)

  # Build respondent-level dataset
  person_s27 <- df_s27 %>%
    group_by(prim_key) %>%
    summarise(
      agegroup = first(agegroup),
      ever_moved_out = max(Moved_out_of_Bay_w23, na.rm = TRUE),
      ever_moved_in  = max(Moved_in_Bay_w23, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      ever_moved_out = ifelse(is.infinite(ever_moved_out), 0, ever_moved_out),
      ever_moved_in  = ifelse(is.infinite(ever_moved_in), 0, ever_moved_in),
      move_status = case_when(
        ever_moved_out == 1 ~ "Out of Bay",
        ever_moved_in == 1  ~ "Within Bay",
        TRUE                ~ "Non-Mover"
      )
    )

  # Merge wave-1 baseline characteristics
  w1_s27 <- df_s27 %>% filter(WAVE == 1) %>%
    select(prim_key, female, age, black_race, asian_race, latino_eth,
           BA_or_greater, Owns_home, Full_Time, Marr_Cohab,
           Kin_in_HH_N, Kin_within_60_N, Kin_GT_Hour_N,
           Nonkin_in_HH_N, Nonkin_within_60_N, Nonkin_GT_Hour_N)

  person_s27 <- person_s27 %>% inner_join(w1_s27, by = "prim_key")

  fmt_pct_s <- function(x) sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  fmt_ms_s  <- function(x) sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))

  build_col_s <- function(data) {
    n <- nrow(data)
    c(
      fmt_pct_s(data$female), fmt_ms_s(data$age),
      fmt_pct_s(data$black_race), fmt_pct_s(data$asian_race), fmt_pct_s(data$latino_eth),
      fmt_pct_s(data$BA_or_greater), fmt_pct_s(data$Owns_home),
      fmt_pct_s(data$Full_Time), fmt_pct_s(data$Marr_Cohab),
      fmt_ms_s(data$Kin_in_HH_N), fmt_ms_s(data$Kin_within_60_N),
      fmt_ms_s(data$Kin_GT_Hour_N), fmt_ms_s(data$Nonkin_in_HH_N),
      fmt_ms_s(data$Nonkin_within_60_N), fmt_ms_s(data$Nonkin_GT_Hour_N),
      as.character(n)
    )
  }

  var_names_s <- c(
    "Female (%)", "Age (mean, SD)",
    "Black (%)", "Asian (%)", "Latino/a (%)",
    "BA or Higher (%)", "Homeowner (%)",
    "Full-Time Employed (%)", "Married/Cohabiting (%)",
    "Kin in Household", "Kin within 60 min",
    "Kin >60 min", "Non-kin in Household",
    "Non-kin within 60 min", "Non-kin >60 min",
    "N (respondents)"
  )

  sec_labels_s <- c("Demographics", "Socioeconomic Status (Baseline)",
                     "Network Variables at Baseline (mean, SD)")

  yn <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 0, move_status == "Non-Mover"))
  yl <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 0, move_status == "Within Bay"))
  yd <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 0, move_status == "Out of Bay"))
  on <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 1, move_status == "Non-Mover"))
  ol <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 1, move_status == "Within Bay"))
  od <- build_col_s(person_s27 %>% filter(agegroup == 1, move_status == "Out of Bay"))

  rows_s <- list()
  for (i in seq_along(var_names_s)) {
    if (i == 1)  rows_s[[length(rows_s) + 1]] <- c(sec_labels_s[1], rep("", 6))
    if (i == 6)  rows_s[[length(rows_s) + 1]] <- c(sec_labels_s[2], rep("", 6))
    if (i == 10) rows_s[[length(rows_s) + 1]] <- c(sec_labels_s[3], rep("", 6))
    rows_s[[length(rows_s) + 1]] <- c(
      paste0("  ", var_names_s[i]), yn[i], yl[i], yd[i], on[i], ol[i], od[i]
    )
  }

  tbl_s <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, rows_s), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(tbl_s) <- c("Variable", "YN", "YL", "YD", "ON", "OL", "OD")

  sec_s <- which(tbl_s$Variable %in% sec_labels_s)
  n_s <- which(grepl("N \\(resp", tbl_s$Variable))

  ft_s <- flextable(tbl_s)
  ft_s <- set_header_labels(ft_s, Variable = "",
    YN = "Non-Mover", YL = "Local", YD = "Distant",
    ON = "Non-Mover", OL = "Local", OD = "Distant")
  ft_s <- add_header_row(ft_s,
    values = c("", "Young Adults (21-30)", "Older Adults (50-70)"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3, 3), top = TRUE)
  ft_s <- theme_booktabs(ft_s)
  ft_s <- bold(ft_s, i = sec_s, j = 1, part = "body")
  if (length(n_s) > 0) {
    ft_s <- hline(ft_s, i = n_s - 1, part = "body", border = fp_border(width = 0.5))
  }
  ft_s <- fontsize(ft_s, size = 9, part = "all")
  ft_s <- font(ft_s, fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all")
  ft_s <- align(ft_s, j = 1, align = "left", part = "body")
  ft_s <- align(ft_s, j = -1, align = "center", part = "body")
  ft_s <- align(ft_s, align = "center", part = "header")
  ft_s <- bold(ft_s, part = "header")
  ft_s <- set_caption(ft_s, caption = "Table S27. Baseline (Wave 1) Characteristics by Subsequent Mobility Status and Age Group (Respondent-Level)")
  ft_s <- add_footer_lines(ft_s, values = paste(
    "Respondent-level: one row per person. Move status based on whether the respondent",
    "ever moved out of the Bay Area, ever moved locally, or never moved during the study.",
    "All characteristics measured at wave 1 (baseline).",
    "Percentages for binary variables; mean (SD) for continuous variables."
  ))
  ft_s <- fontsize(ft_s, size = 9, part = "footer")
  ft_s <- font(ft_s, fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "footer")
  ft_s <- italic(ft_s, part = "footer")
  ft_s <- autofit(ft_s)
  ft_s
}
```
### F.2 Variable Definitions

#### Moving Variables

**Moved_out_of_Bay_w23**

**Definition:** Binary indicator (0/1) for whether the respondent moved out of the Bay Area between survey waves.

**Source:** Derived from survey question `a19j` (waves 2-3 only):

> "How far is where you live now from where you lived at the time of the previous interview? Is it..."
>
> 1. In the same neighborhood
> 2. In a different neighborhood but in the same town
> 3. In a different town but still in the Bay Area
> 4. Out of the Bay Area, but still in California
> 5. Out of California
> 6. Other (recoded into categories 1-5)

**Coding:** `Moved_out_of_Bay_w23 = 1` if response is 4 (Out of Bay, in California) or 5 (Out of California). Otherwise 0.

**Wave 1 handling:** Wave 1 does not have question a19j. Instead, `a19c_onlyw1` (time at current residence) is used: respondents at residence ≤2 years are coded as moved (1), those ≥3 years as not moved (0).

**Corrections:** Five cases were recoded from 1 to 0 at Wave 3 based on qualitative review of open-ended responses indicating local moves rather than out-of-Bay moves.

**Moved_in_Bay_w23**

**Definition:** Binary indicator (0/1) for whether the respondent moved within the Bay Area between survey waves.

**Source:** Derived from `a19j` responses 2 (different neighborhood, same town) or 3 (different town, still in Bay Area).

**Moved_w23**

**Definition:** Binary indicator (0/1) for any move between survey waves, regardless of distance.

**Source:** Derived from `a19j` responses 1-5 (any move reported).
